channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Senior Research Manager Position at Harvard University: Join the Center for Education Policy Research
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Harvard University is seeking an experienced Senior Research Manager to join the Center for Education Policy Research (CEPR) at the Harvard Graduate School of Education (HGSE). This opportunity is ideal for professionals with a strong background in quantitative research, education policy, and data analysis who are passionate about improving education systems through evidence-based decision-making. The […]

The post Senior Research Manager Position at Harvard University: Join the Center for Education Policy Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Harvard University is seeking an experienced Senior Research Manager to join the Center for Education Policy Research (CEPR) at the Harvard Graduate School of Education (HGSE). This opportunity is ideal for professionals with a strong background in quantitative research, education policy, and data analysis who are passionate about improving education systems through evidence-based decision-making.

The position offers a competitive salary ranging from $100,000 to $105,000 per year, a hybrid work structure, and the opportunity to collaborate with education leaders, researchers, and policymakers around the world. The role focuses on managing high-impact research projects that support education policy development and school improvement initiatives.

About Harvard University and the Harvard Graduate School of Education

Working at Harvard University means joining a global academic community committed to advancing knowledge, fostering innovation, and addressing some of the world’s most pressing challenges. Harvard promotes a collaborative and inclusive workplace where employees can develop their skills while contributing to meaningful research and societal progress.

The Harvard Graduate School of Education (HGSE) is widely recognized for its dedication to transforming education through research, teaching, and policy leadership. HGSE brings together educators, scholars, and professionals who share a common goal: improving educational opportunities and outcomes for learners worldwide.

Many professionals choose HGSE because of its strong mission-driven environment, outstanding leadership, and vibrant community that values diversity, collaboration, and intellectual growth.

About the Center for Education Policy Research (CEPR)

The Center for Education Policy Research (CEPR) plays a crucial role in advancing education reform through data-driven research. The center collaborates with school districts, charter school networks, state education agencies, and international partners to develop rigorous research that informs education policy and strategic decision-making.

CEPR’s core philosophy is that education leaders should rely on evidence rather than assumptions when making decisions that affect students, teachers, and school systems. By applying advanced research methods and data analytics, the center helps education institutions understand what works, what does not, and how systems can be improved.

Through innovative research models and partnerships, CEPR aims to transform education systems and ensure that all students have the opportunity to learn and thrive.

Job Summary: Senior Research Manager

The Senior Research Manager (SRM) will play a central role in managing and advancing CEPR’s research initiatives. The position involves overseeing analytical work across multiple projects, guiding research teams, and ensuring the quality and accuracy of all research outputs.

One of the key responsibilities of the role will involve managing projects related to a research partnership with an education nonprofit organization in the Philippines and its government partners. These projects aim to support school improvement efforts and develop a long-term research agenda that strengthens education systems.

In addition to managing international projects, the Senior Research Manager may contribute to other CEPR initiatives and supervise Research Analysts across multiple studies.

Key Responsibilities

The Senior Research Manager will oversee the full research lifecycle, from project design to publication. Major responsibilities include the following:

Leading Research and Analytics

The SRM will lead the analytical work across several research projects, including:

  • Developing research questions and study designs

  • Writing grant proposals and securing funding

  • Managing data collection and analysis

  • Producing reports and academic publications

The role requires strong expertise in quantitative methods and statistical analysis to ensure research findings are accurate and impactful.

Managing Research Teams

The Senior Research Manager will supervise Research Analysts and support their professional development. This includes mentoring analysts, providing training in research methods, and organizing workshops to strengthen analytical and technical skills.

Team leadership is a central aspect of the role, ensuring that research staff develop their expertise while contributing effectively to ongoing projects.

Training and Technical Support

Another major component of the role involves providing technical guidance on research methods and statistical techniques. The SRM will train both internal team members and external education agency staff on selecting appropriate analytical approaches and using data tools effectively.

Project Planning and Coordination

The Senior Research Manager will be responsible for ensuring that research projects are completed successfully and on schedule. Key tasks include:

  • Creating project work plans

  • Coordinating team activities across multiple research initiatives

  • Managing project deadlines

  • Leading collaborative meetings between researchers, project directors, and principal investigators

The role also requires working closely with leadership teams and external partners to develop strategies for future research initiatives.

Ensuring Research Quality and Integrity

Maintaining high research standards is essential for CEPR’s mission. The Senior Research Manager will design rigorous studies, identify potential weaknesses in research designs, and ensure that all analyses meet strict quality standards.

Responsibilities also include reviewing statistical code, verifying analytical outputs, and establishing protocols that ensure research findings are reproducible and transparent.

Conducting Advanced Data Analysis

The SRM will also contribute directly to analytical work when necessary, including complex statistical programming and data analysis. This involves managing large-scale education datasets and using advanced statistical tools to answer critical research questions.

Publishing and Presenting Research

The Senior Research Manager will contribute to research publications and present findings at conferences, meetings, and professional forums. Communicating research insights to both technical and non-technical audiences is a key responsibility of the position.

Required Qualifications

Applicants must meet several core qualifications to be considered for the role.

Educational Background

Candidates must hold an advanced degree (master’s level or higher) in one of the following fields:

  • Education

  • Public Policy

  • Economics

  • Statistics

Alternatively, candidates with at least five years of directly related professional experience may also qualify.

Professional Experience

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • At least two years of management experience leading a team

  • A minimum of five years of experience in quantitative research, including data management, analysis, and report writing

  • Strong experience with Stata statistical software

  • Experience working with large-scale education datasets

Candidates should also demonstrate the ability to manage multiple complex projects simultaneously and effectively communicate research findings to diverse audiences.

Preferred Qualifications

Although not mandatory, several additional qualifications are highly desirable for this role.

These include:

  • A PhD in quantitative social science methods

  • Experience training others in data analysis and programming

  • Experience collaborating with state education agencies, K–12 school districts, or higher education institutions

  • Knowledge of program evaluation methods, including causal inference and impact evaluation

  • Experience managing research partnerships and engaging stakeholders

  • Experience completing full research projects from concept to publication

  • Additional programming expertise in R or Python

Candidates who can translate technical findings into accessible materials for non-technical audiences will have a significant advantage.

APPLY  HERE

Recruitment Process

Candidates who pass the initial screening stage will be required to complete several evaluation steps, including:

  • A case study assessment

  • Submission of sample Stata code

  • A writing sample

  • Academic transcripts

Final-stage candidates will also present their previous research work.

Applicants must submit a cover letter and resume combined into a single PDF document, clearly stating that they meet the required qualifications.

Work Structure and Location

The Senior Research Manager position follows a hybrid work model. Employees will work:

  • Three days per week in the Cambridge office

  • Two days remotely

The work schedule and location may be adjusted based on operational needs and university policies.

It is important to note that Harvard University cannot provide visa sponsorship for this position.

Salary and Contract Details

The position offers a competitive compensation package.

Key details include:

  • Salary: $100,000 – $105,000 annually, depending on experience

  • Work schedule: 35 hours per week

  • Contract duration: One-year term appointment with strong possibility of renewal

  • Salary grade: Level 057

Benefits and Employee Support

Harvard provides a comprehensive benefits package designed to support employees’ wellbeing and professional development.

Key benefits include:

  • Generous paid leave and parental leave

  • Medical, dental, and vision insurance starting on the first day of employment

  • Retirement plans with university contributions

  • Mental health and wellbeing resources

  • Support programs for families and caregivers

  • Tuition assistance and professional development opportunities

  • Commuter benefits and campus discounts

These benefits help employees maintain a healthy balance between their professional responsibilities and personal lives.

Equal Opportunity Commitment

Harvard University is committed to maintaining a workplace that is inclusive, equitable, and free from discrimination. The university actively seeks talent from diverse backgrounds and ensures that all employees are treated fairly regardless of race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, religion, disability, veteran status, or other protected characteristics.

This commitment to diversity strengthens Harvard’s academic mission and supports a thriving academic community.

Conclusion

The Senior Research Manager position at Harvard University’s Center for Education Policy Research presents a unique opportunity for experienced researchers to contribute to meaningful education reforms. By combining rigorous research methods with real-world partnerships, the role allows professionals to influence education policies and practices at both national and international levels.

With competitive compensation, a supportive work environment, and the opportunity to collaborate with leading scholars and policymakers, this role is well suited for individuals committed to improving education through research and data-driven decision-making.

Discover more similar Opportunities here. 

The post Senior Research Manager Position at Harvard University: Join the Center for Education Policy Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Princess Sabeeka Global Award for Women’s Empowerment 2026: A $100,000 Global Opportunity Recognizing Leaders Advancing Gender Equality
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Across the world, gender equality and women’s empowerment remain central pillars for sustainable development, social progress, and inclusive economic growth. To recognize outstanding efforts that advance these goals, the Princess Sabeeka Bint Ibrahim Al Khalifa Global Award for Women’s Empowerment was established as one of the most prestigious international recognitions for initiatives that transform the […]

The post Princess Sabeeka Global Award for Women’s Empowerment 2026: A $100,000 Global Opportunity Recognizing Leaders Advancing Gender Equality appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Across the world, gender equality and women’s empowerment remain central pillars for sustainable development, social progress, and inclusive economic growth. To recognize outstanding efforts that advance these goals, the Princess Sabeeka Bint Ibrahim Al Khalifa Global Award for Women’s Empowerment was established as one of the most prestigious international recognitions for initiatives that transform the lives of women and girls.

The award celebrates innovative programs, impactful policies, and visionary leadership that contribute to empowering women across different sectors of society. Through this global initiative, governments, organizations, and individuals working to promote gender equality receive international recognition, financial support, and a platform to showcase their impact.

 

With four awards worth $100,000 each, the program highlights successful models that demonstrate how women’s empowerment contributes to sustainable development and stronger societies worldwide.

About the Princess Sabeeka Global Award for Women’s Empowerment

The Princess Sabeeka Global Award for Women’s Empowerment was launched by the Kingdom of Bahrain in partnership with UN Women to celebrate initiatives that empower women and girls around the world. The award recognizes innovative approaches and successful projects that promote gender equality in public policy, social development, and economic participation.

The award is designed to:

  • Highlight the contributions of individuals and organizations advancing women’s empowerment.

  • Encourage creative and innovative solutions that support gender equality.

  • Recognize successful programs that promote women’s participation in society.

  • Inspire global collaboration toward inclusive development and equality.

By showcasing powerful initiatives and leaders who are making a difference, the award aims to strengthen international momentum toward achieving gender equality and sustainable development.

Prize Value and Benefits

One of the most attractive aspects of this opportunity is the financial and reputational reward that comes with winning the award.

Each selected winner receives:

  • US$100,000 prize money

  • Global recognition for their work

  • Increased international visibility

  • Opportunities to showcase their initiative on global platforms

  • Enhanced credibility for scaling and expanding their programs

 

There are four winners, each representing a different category of contribution toward women’s empowerment.

Award Categories

The Princess Sabeeka Global Award recognizes achievements across four main sectors:

1. Public Sector – This category recognizes government institutions and public agencies that have implemented policies or programs that significantly advance women’s empowerment and gender equality.

2. Private Sector – Private companies and business organizations that actively promote gender equality within their workforce, leadership structures, and corporate strategies can apply under this category.

3. Civil Society – Non-governmental organizations, community groups, foundations, and civil society networks that advocate for women’s rights and empowerment are eligible here.

4. Individual Champions – This category celebrates outstanding individuals who have dedicated their careers or initiatives to promoting women’s empowerment and gender equality.

Each category recognizes innovative and impactful initiatives that demonstrate measurable results in advancing women’s rights and opportunities.

Who Can Apply

The award is open to applicants worldwide and encourages participation from individuals and institutions that have implemented meaningful initiatives aimed at empowering women.

Eligible applicants include:

  • Individual advocates for women’s empowerment

  • Civil society organizations and NGOs

  • Government institutions and public sector bodies

  • Private sector companies and corporate initiatives

Applicants must demonstrate measurable impact through programs, initiatives, or policies that support women’s empowerment and gender equality.

Countries Eligible to Apply

This is a global award, meaning applicants from all countries and regions are welcome to apply.

Eligible regions include:

  • Africa

  • Asia

  • Europe

  • North America

  • South America

  • The Middle East

  • Oceania

The award aims to recognize impactful initiatives from both developed and developing countries, highlighting diverse solutions to gender equality challenges worldwide.

Qualifications and Eligibility Criteria

While the award is open to a wide range of applicants, candidates must demonstrate that their initiative or work contributes significantly to women’s empowerment.

Key eligibility considerations include:

1. Demonstrated Impact – Applicants must show tangible results from their initiatives, such as policy reforms, increased participation of women in leadership, economic empowerment programs, or social reforms.

2. Innovative Approaches – The award values innovative and creative solutions that address gender inequality in new and effective ways.

3. Sustainability – Projects and initiatives should demonstrate long-term sustainability and the potential for continued impact.

4. Replicability – Programs that can be replicated in other countries or communities may receive strong consideration, as they help scale solutions globally.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can submit their application through the official website by following these steps:

  1. Visit the official award website.

  2. Navigate to the Apply Here section.

  3. Select the appropriate award category.

  4. Complete the application form with details about your initiative.

  5. Submit supporting documentation demonstrating impact and results.

Applications must be completed online via the official platform.

Selection Process

Applications submitted for the Princess Sabeeka Global Award undergo a rigorous evaluation process conducted by an international jury composed of experts in gender equality and development.

The evaluation focuses on:

  • Innovation and creativity

  • Demonstrated impact

  • Sustainability and scalability

  • Contribution to gender equality and women’s empowerment

The jury includes internationally recognized experts, policymakers, and leaders working in the field of women’s rights and empowerment.

Why This Award Matters

Gender equality remains one of the most critical development challenges globally. Despite significant progress, women still face barriers in education, employment, leadership, and access to resources.

Awards such as the Princess Sabeeka Global Award play an important role by:

  • Recognizing leaders driving positive change

  • Encouraging governments and organizations to adopt gender-inclusive policies

  • Promoting successful models of women’s empowerment

  • Amplifying global awareness of gender equality initiatives

By highlighting successful programs and visionary leaders, the award contributes to the broader international effort to achieve sustainable development and inclusive growth.

Deadline

Applicants must submit their applications before:

20th March 2026 (11:59 PM New York Time)

The Princess Sabeeka Global Award for Women’s Empowerment represents one of the world’s most important recognitions for initiatives that transform the lives of women and girls. By offering substantial financial support and international recognition, the award encourages governments, organizations, and individuals to develop innovative solutions that advance gender equality.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Princess Sabeeka Global Award for Women’s Empowerment 2026: A $100,000 Global Opportunity Recognizing Leaders Advancing Gender Equality appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Empowering Global Women in Management Program 2026 (GWIM 76) in Washington DC
item.dc:creator: Michelle Zyongwe
item.description:
The Empowering Global Women in Management Program 2026 (GWIM 76) is an exceptional leadership development opportunity designed to strengthen the economic leadership and management capacity of women around the world. Implemented by Counterpart International with generous support from the ExxonMobil Foundation, this prestigious program equips women leaders with the knowledge, networks, and tools needed to […]

The post Empowering Global Women in Management Program 2026 (GWIM 76) in Washington DC appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Empowering Global Women in Management Program 2026 (GWIM 76) is an exceptional leadership development opportunity designed to strengthen the economic leadership and management capacity of women around the world. Implemented by Counterpart International with generous support from the ExxonMobil Foundation, this prestigious program equips women leaders with the knowledge, networks, and tools needed to advance inclusive economic growth and social change.

Launched in 2005, the Global Women in Management Program (GWIM) has become a leading initiative dedicated to building the leadership capacity of women working in civil society organizations, social enterprises, and institutions that support women’s economic empowerment.

The GWIM 76 cohort for 2026 will bring together 20 women leaders from across the globe for an intensive hybrid leadership program that combines in-person training in Washington, DC with ongoing online mentorship and learning sessions.

The program aims to address persistent barriers faced by women in economic leadership by strengthening decision-making capacity, increasing access to professional networks and resources, and enhancing the ability of women leaders to grow sustainable enterprises and organizations.

The application deadline is April 30, 2026.

Global Leadership Opportunity Through GWIM

The Global Women in Management Program is designed as a long-term investment in women’s leadership development.

The program integrates leadership training, executive coaching, mentorship, and global networking opportunities to support women leaders who are working to expand economic opportunities for women in their communities.

GWIM builds on over 40 years of experience in women’s leadership development, combining practical management training with innovative approaches that help participants adapt to rapidly evolving economic environments.

Participants learn how to navigate complex organizational challenges, build sustainable programs, and influence policies that support women’s economic empowerment.

Hybrid Learning Structure of the GWIM Program

The GWIM 76 program will take place through a hybrid learning model that includes both in-person and online sessions.

In-Person Leadership Training

The program will begin with an intensive two-week leadership training in Washington, DC from October 19 to October 30, 2026.

During this period, participants will engage with expert facilitators, international development professionals, and fellow women leaders from around the world.

This immersive training experience includes leadership workshops, collaborative learning sessions, and networking opportunities.

Participants will also gain exposure to global perspectives on women’s economic advancement and leadership.

Online Learning and Mentorship

Following the in-person training, participants will continue their learning through online training sessions, mentorship programs, and collaborative meetings.

These sessions will take place from December 2026 to May 2027, totaling approximately 16 to 20 hours of online engagement.

Online sessions will focus on leadership development, technology integration, and strategies for organizational growth.

Core Learning Areas in the GWIM Program

The program curriculum focuses on strengthening leadership capabilities and improving organizational performance.

Key learning areas include:

Leadership Development

Participants will explore different dimensions of leadership, including personal leadership style, emotional intelligence, and confidence-building strategies.

The program encourages participants to reflect on their leadership approach while developing strategies for influencing positive change within their organizations.

Institutional Sustainability

The program provides insights into donor engagement, fundraising strategies, and financial sustainability models that help organizations maintain long-term impact.

Participants will also learn how to cultivate and sustain relationships with donors and partners.

Economic Advancement

Participants will develop strategic planning skills that support business and organizational growth.

The program incorporates tools such as the Business Canvas Model to help leaders design scalable and sustainable initiatives.

Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technologies

The program introduces participants to AI-enabled learning tools and digital platforms that can improve research, data analysis, and innovation.

Participants will also explore ethical considerations surrounding artificial intelligence and technology-driven economic transformation.

Practicum and Action Plans

Each participant will design a personalized action plan to implement leadership and organizational development strategies within their workplace.

These action plans allow participants to apply their new skills directly to real-world challenges.

Post-Program Mentorship and Global Network

One of the most valuable aspects of the GWIM program is the long-term support available to participants after completing the training.

Each participant is paired with a GWIM alumnae mentor who provides guidance on implementing leadership action plans and addressing professional challenges.

Graduates also join a global network of more than 1,000 GWIM alumnae, creating opportunities for collaboration, knowledge sharing, and partnerships across countries.

The program also provides continued access to:

  • Leadership resources
  • Training opportunities
  • Fellowship programs
  • Funding opportunities

These networks support ongoing leadership development and professional advancement.

Who Should Apply for the GWIM Program

The Global Women in Management Program is specifically designed for early- to mid-career women leaders working in organizations that support women’s economic empowerment.

The program targets professionals working in:

  • Local NGOs
  • Cooperatives
  • Businesswomen’s associations
  • Microcredit organizations
  • Social enterprises
  • STEAM initiatives supporting women and girls

Participants should have experience designing or implementing programs that promote economic opportunities for women.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Minimum five years of experience in development programming
  • Minimum three years of experience working on women’s economic empowerment initiatives
  • Strong English language proficiency
  • Ability to participate fully in in-person and online sessions

Participants must also be available to travel to Washington, DC for the two-week in-person training.

Eligible Countries

Women from the following countries are eligible to apply:

Brazil

Egypt

Guyana

India

Indonesia

Kazakhstan

Mexico

Mozambique

China

Papua New Guinea

Qatar

Saudi Arabia

Vietnam

United Arab Emirates

Application Process

Applicants must complete the online application form and submit the required documents.

As part of the application process, candidates must also provide a Supervisor Endorsement Form confirming support from their organization.

The endorsement form may be submitted after the application deadline but must be received by May 5, 2026.

Deadline for Applications

All applications must be submitted by 11:59 PM EST on April 30, 2026.

Late applications will not be considered.

Learn More About This Opportunity

To learn more about the Empowering Global Women in Management Program 2026 (GWIM 76) opportunity and access the official application page, click here.

APPLY HERE


Conclusion

The Global Women in Management Program 2026 offers an exceptional opportunity for women leaders working in economic empowerment and social development to strengthen their leadership capabilities and expand their global networks.

Through intensive training, mentorship, and collaboration with women leaders from around the world, participants gain the skills and confidence needed to advance economic opportunities for women and drive meaningful social change.

Discover More Fellowship Opportunities

For more fellowship opportunities on OFY Opportunities, click here.

The post Empowering Global Women in Management Program 2026 (GWIM 76) in Washington DC appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Transformative Leadership Director Job 2026 at Teach For All – Global Leadership Accelerator
item.dc:creator: Michelle Zyongwe
item.description:
The Transformative Leadership Director Job 2026 at Teach For All’s Global Leadership Accelerator (GLA) presents a remarkable opportunity for experienced professionals who are passionate about reshaping education systems globally. This remote leadership position allows individuals to work with one of the world’s most influential education networks while supporting thousands of leaders dedicated to creating equitable […]

The post Transformative Leadership Director Job 2026 at Teach For All – Global Leadership Accelerator appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Transformative Leadership Director Job 2026 at Teach For All’s Global Leadership Accelerator (GLA) presents a remarkable opportunity for experienced professionals who are passionate about reshaping education systems globally. This remote leadership position allows individuals to work with one of the world’s most influential education networks while supporting thousands of leaders dedicated to creating equitable learning opportunities for children.

Teach For All is a global network of more than 60 independent organizations, supporting over 15,000 teachers currently working in classrooms and nearly 125,000 alumni leaders across multiple countries. These leaders collectively impact millions of children worldwide, working to address systemic inequities in education.

The Leadership Director for the Global Leadership Accelerator will play a crucial role in designing leadership development experiences, coaching senior leaders, and shaping global initiatives that accelerate system-wide change. Through this position, the selected candidate will work with social entrepreneurs, educators, policymakers, and changemakers across the globe.

This job opportunity is remote and available to candidates located in several Asia-Pacific countries, including Bangladesh, Hong Kong, India, Indonesia, Malaysia, Pakistan, the Philippines, Singapore, and Thailand.

Leadership Role in the Global Leadership Accelerator

The Leadership Director will work within the Global Leadership Accelerator, a strategic initiative that focuses on cultivating strong leadership across the Teach For All network.

The Accelerator aims to build deeply conscious leaders who are motivated by purpose and committed to transforming inequitable systems in education. By developing leadership capacity among educators, social entrepreneurs, and policymakers, the program seeks to drive meaningful change in global education systems.

This role also connects closely with the Global Institute for Shaping a Better Future, an initiative that facilitates learning and collaboration among education leaders, researchers, and policymakers around the world.

The institute contributes to the development of leadership skills while also generating insights, research, and tools that support the transformation of education systems globally.

Key Responsibilities of the Leadership Director

The Leadership Director will help launch new initiatives and expand existing leadership development programs across the Teach For All network.

Some of the most important responsibilities include designing leadership experiences and supporting senior leaders who are working to reshape education systems.

Key responsibilities include:

Designing Leadership Programs

The selected candidate will help co-design leadership development initiatives that bring together emerging and established leaders across the global education sector.

Examples include:

  • Designing fellowships for policymakers and political leaders
  • Creating leadership development programs for educators and system leaders
  • Facilitating global learning experiences and workshops

Coaching Senior Leaders

The Leadership Director will coach and support senior leaders within the Teach For All network.

This involves:

  • Leading peer learning groups
  • Supporting leadership teams in strengthening their impact
  • Providing guidance on leadership development and strategic thinking

Facilitating Global Learning Experiences

Another important responsibility involves facilitating workshops and leadership programs for educators and changemakers worldwide.

These learning experiences may include both in-person and virtual formats.

Managing Strategic Leadership Initiatives

The Leadership Director will manage projects from conception to execution.

This includes:

  • Program design
  • Communications and marketing
  • Participant enrollment
  • Program delivery
  • Monitoring impact and outcomes

Building Partnerships

The role also involves developing partnerships with external organizations and collaborators to co-create flagship leadership programs.

These partnerships help expand the reach and effectiveness of leadership development initiatives.

Qualifications Required for the Leadership Director Role

Teach For All is seeking experienced professionals with strong expertise in leadership development and organizational transformation.

The ideal candidate will possess:

  • Extensive experience in leadership development or organizational development
  • Strong coaching and facilitation skills
  • Experience working with senior leaders or executive teams
  • Expertise in designing learning experiences for adults
  • Exceptional strategic thinking and problem-solving abilities

Candidates should also demonstrate the ability to work effectively in global environments and collaborate with diverse teams across cultures and regions.

Additional Experience and Skills

While not mandatory, the following experience is considered highly valuable:

  • Background in education leadership or school leadership
  • Experience in community organizing or systems change
  • Expertise in diversity, equity, and inclusion initiatives
  • Experience designing programs at scale
  • Familiarity with the Teach For All network or one of its partner organizations

Teach For All is particularly interested in candidates who demonstrate an entrepreneurial mindset and the ability to envision and launch innovative leadership programs.

Work Environment and Travel Expectations

This role is fully remote, allowing professionals to work from one of several eligible countries across the Asia-Pacific region.

Although the position is primarily virtual, the role may require up to 20% travel to support leadership programs, workshops, or global learning initiatives.

Because the organization operates globally, team members may occasionally need to work across different time zones.

Benefits of the Leadership Director Role

Teach For All offers a supportive work environment and a strong commitment to staff well-being and development.

Benefits include:

  • Opportunities for professional growth and leadership development
  • Flexible work arrangements and remote work
  • Generous leave policies
  • Comprehensive employee benefits
  • Opportunities to contribute to global education transformation

Employees also become part of a collaborative global network of education leaders dedicated to ensuring every child has access to quality education.

Application Process

Interested candidates should submit the following documents:

  • Resume or Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • A one-page motivation letter written in English

The motivation letter should explain the applicant’s interest in the role and how their experience aligns with the responsibilities of the position.

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early applications are strongly encouraged.

Work Authorization Requirement

Teach For All does not provide visa sponsorship or immigration assistance for this role.

Applicants must already have legal authorization to work in the country where they are applying.

Learn More About This Opportunity

To learn more about the Transformative Leadership Director Job 2026 at Teach For All’s Global Leadership Accelerator, visit the official application page here.

APPLY HERE

Conclusion

The Leadership Director position within Teach For All’s Global Leadership Accelerator represents an exceptional opportunity for experienced professionals who are passionate about education transformation and leadership development.

Through this role, the selected candidate will contribute to shaping leadership across one of the largest global networks working to ensure that all children have access to quality education.

Discover More Job Opportunities

For more job opportunities on OFY Opportunities, click here.

The post Transformative Leadership Director Job 2026 at Teach For All – Global Leadership Accelerator appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded 2026 DTH-Lab Research Fellowship for Students and Early-Career Professionals
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
The Digital Transformations for Health Lab (DTH-Lab) has officially announced the 2026 Research Fellowship Programme, a highly competitive global opportunity designed to empower emerging researchers and professionals interested in digital health, public policy, and technological transformation in healthcare systems. This prestigious fellowship provides students and early-career professionals from low- and middle-income countries (LMICs) the chance […]

The post Fully Funded 2026 DTH-Lab Research Fellowship for Students and Early-Career Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Digital Transformations for Health Lab (DTH-Lab) has officially announced the 2026 Research Fellowship Programme, a highly competitive global opportunity designed to empower emerging researchers and professionals interested in digital health, public policy, and technological transformation in healthcare systems.

This prestigious fellowship provides students and early-career professionals from low- and middle-income countries (LMICs) the chance to contribute to cutting-edge research projects shaping the future of digital health governance and policy worldwide.

The fellowship is part of the organization’s mission to develop the next generation of global health leaders capable of navigating the complex intersection of technology, public health, and global governance. Through this program, selected fellows will collaborate with leading experts, contribute to meaningful research initiatives, and help shape innovative policies for digital health transformation.

About the Digital Transformations for Health Lab (DTH-Lab)

The Digital Transformations for Health Lab is a global consortium that works at the intersection of digital technology, public health systems, and governance. The organization focuses on implementing recommendations from the Lancet and Financial Times Commission on Governing Health Futures 2030, emphasizing value-driven and youth-centered digital health transformations.

DTH-Lab collaborates with policymakers, academic institutions, global health organizations, and youth leaders to advance innovative approaches that improve health outcomes through technology.

Its work focuses on:

  • Digital health policy and governance

  • Health data systems and public health technology

  • Youth participation in global health transformation

  • AI and emerging technologies in healthcare

  • Digital well-being and technology-driven public health solutions

Through fellowships like this one, the organization builds the capacity of future health leaders who can guide governments and institutions through the rapidly evolving digital healthcare landscape.

Purpose of the DTH-Lab Research Fellowship Programme

The DTH-Lab Research Fellowship was designed to nurture young professionals who want to influence how digital technologies shape public health policies and healthcare systems.

The fellowship program has three major goals:

1. Build the Capacity of Future Health Leaders

Participants will gain hands-on experience conducting research related to digital health governance and innovation. The program equips fellows with critical analytical, research, and policy skills needed in global health leadership.

2. Advance Research on Digital Health Transformation

Fellows will contribute to research projects that explore how digital technologies influence healthcare systems, health equity, and youth well-being.

3. Promote Youth Engagement in Health Policy

The fellowship encourages young professionals to play a meaningful role in shaping digital health strategies at national and international levels.

Ultimately, the program aims to develop a new generation of researchers and policymakers capable of addressing the ethical, technological, and societal implications of digital health systems.

Research Topics for the 2026 Fellowship

For the 2026 cohort, selected fellows will work on research projects addressing some of the most urgent issues in digital health transformation.

Key research themes include:

1. National Policies for Young People’s Digital Well-Being

This research explores how governments design policies that protect young people in digital environments while promoting positive digital experiences and mental well-being.

2. Digital Determinants of Health in Africa

This topic examines how digital access, connectivity, data governance, and technology infrastructure influence public health outcomes across the African continent.

3. Digital Citizenship for Health

This research focuses on building frameworks that enable individuals to responsibly use digital technologies to improve personal and community health.

4. Artificial Intelligence and the Health Workforce

Participants will examine how AI technologies are transforming healthcare delivery and redefining roles within the healthcare workforce.

5. Emerging Roles in an AI-Enabled Health Workforce

This research investigates how healthcare professionals must adapt as artificial intelligence becomes integrated into clinical practice and health systems.

Each fellow will contribute to a specific research area, producing insights that may inform global digital health policies.

Who Can Apply

The DTH-Lab Research Fellowship Programme 2026 is open to a specific group of individuals with strong interest in digital health, public health research, and policy development.

Eligible Applicants

Applicants must fall into one of the following categories:

  • University students

  • Early-career researchers

  • Young professionals in health, policy, or technology sectors

  • Professionals interested in digital health transformation

  • Researchers exploring digital governance or AI in healthcare

The program targets individuals who are passionate about shaping the future of healthcare through digital innovation and research.

Apply here

Countries and Regions Eligible to Apply

The fellowship is open to applicants from low- and middle-income countries (LMICs) around the world.

This means candidates may apply from regions including:

  • Africa

  • Asia

  • Latin America

  • The Caribbean

  • Middle East

  • Other LMIC regions globally

Applicants must be based in one of these eligible countries at the time of application.

Key Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for the DTH-Lab Research Fellowship Programme, applicants should meet several important criteria.

1. Academic or Professional Background

Applicants should have a background in fields such as:

  • Public health

  • Health policy

  • Digital health

  • Technology and data science

  • Global health

  • Public policy

  • Artificial intelligence in healthcare

  • Health governance

  • Development studies

2. Career Stage

The fellowship specifically targets:

  • Students

  • Early-career researchers

  • Young professionals at the beginning stages of their careers

3. Interest in Digital Health Governance

Applicants must demonstrate a clear interest in the intersection of:

  • Technology

  • Health systems

  • Policy development

  • Digital governance

4. Research and Analytical Skills

Candidates should have strong research and analytical capabilities, as the fellowship focuses heavily on policy analysis and academic research.

Benefits of the Fellowship

Being selected as a DTH-Lab Research Fellow provides numerous academic and professional advantages.

1. Global Research Experience – Fellows will work on impactful projects exploring the future of digital health systems.

2. Collaboration With Global Experts – Participants will collaborate with researchers, policymakers, and international health experts.

3. Career Development – The program helps fellows develop key competencies such as:

  • Research methodology

  • Policy analysis

  • Health governance expertise

  • International collaboration skills

4. Exposure to Global Health Policy – Fellows gain insight into how digital technologies influence healthcare governance and policy decisions worldwide.

5. Contribution to Global Digital Health Discussions – Research produced through the fellowship may influence international debates on digital health transformation.

Why This Fellowship Matters

Digital technology is rapidly transforming healthcare systems around the world. From artificial intelligence to telemedicine and digital data systems, technology is reshaping how healthcare services are delivered and governed.

However, these transformations also raise complex questions related to:

  • Ethics

  • Equity

  • Data governance

  • Health access

  • Digital rights

The DTH-Lab Research Fellowship Programme provides a unique platform for emerging leaders to contribute to solving these challenges.

By engaging students and young professionals in meaningful research and policy discussions, the program ensures that future digital health systems are designed to benefit communities worldwide.

Important Dates

  • Applications Open: 30th March 2026

  • Application Deadline: 12th April 2026

The 2026 DTH-Lab Research Fellowship Programme is an exceptional opportunity for students and early-career professionals from low- and middle-income countries to become actively involved in shaping the future of global health systems. Participants will gain valuable research experience, collaborate with global experts, and contribute to discussions that influence how digital technologies transform healthcare worldwide.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Fully Funded 2026 DTH-Lab Research Fellowship for Students and Early-Career Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Young Scholars Invent STEM Program at Rice University 2026: A Powerful Opportunity for High School Students to Build Real-World Engineering Solutions
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
Students passionate about science, engineering, and innovation now have an exceptional opportunity to participate in the Young Scholars Invent program organized by the Office of STEM Engagement at Rice University. This program is designed to immerse high school students in real-world engineering and technology challenges, allowing them to collaborate, design, test, and present innovative solutions. […]

The post Young Scholars Invent STEM Program at Rice University 2026: A Powerful Opportunity for High School Students to Build Real-World Engineering Solutions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Students passionate about science, engineering, and innovation now have an exceptional opportunity to participate in the Young Scholars Invent program organized by the Office of STEM Engagement at Rice University.

This program is designed to immerse high school students in real-world engineering and technology challenges, allowing them to collaborate, design, test, and present innovative solutions. Hosted in Houston, the initiative equips participants with essential research, design, and communication skills that are critical in modern STEM careers.

The program is run by the Rice University Office of STEM Engagement (often known as R-STEM), a unit dedicated to expanding access to high-quality science and technology education for students. Through immersive experiences like Young Scholars Invent, students gain exposure to the engineering design process and learn how to translate creative ideas into practical innovations.

What Is the Young Scholars Invent Program?

The Young Scholars Invent Program is a hands-on STEM workshop that introduces students to collaborative engineering problem-solving and design innovation.

The workshop typically runs for about 1.5 days, during which students work in multidisciplinary teams to solve real-world challenges provided by industry partners and organizations.

Rather than focusing on theoretical learning, the program emphasizes experiential learning through design thinking, experimentation, and teamwork.

Participants go through the entire innovation process:

  • Understanding a real-world problem

  • Researching possible solutions

  • Forming diverse teams

  • Designing and prototyping solutions

  • Testing ideas

  • Presenting their final concept to judges

This immersive experience simulates the collaborative environment used in professional engineering and technology industries.

Why the Young Scholars Invent Program Is Unique

Unlike many STEM competitions that focus on school-versus-school rivalry, the Young Scholars Invent initiative encourages collaboration across schools and backgrounds.

Students are allowed to choose the problem they want to solve and form teams with participants from other schools based on shared interests.

This model reflects how innovation happens in real-world workplaces where professionals from different disciplines collaborate to solve complex problems.

Key distinctive features include:

1. Industry-Simulated Design Experience – Students participate in an industry-style engineering design challenge, gaining insight into how engineers approach real problems.

2. Collaborative Multidisciplinary Teams – Participants form teams composed of students from various schools and backgrounds, encouraging diverse perspectives.

3. Real-World Innovation Challenges – Problems addressed in the program are drawn from industry and agency partners, ensuring relevance and practical value.

4. Emphasis on Communication and Presentation – Students must present their solutions to a panel of judges, strengthening public speaking and technical communication skills.

Program Objectives and Learning Outcomes

The Young Scholars Invent program is designed to cultivate key skills required in modern science and engineering careers.

Students who participate can significantly enhance their abilities in the following areas:

1. Engineering Research Skills – Participants learn how to apply scientific and engineering principles to develop solutions to real-world problems.

2. Problem Identification and Analysis -Students learn to:

  • Identify engineering challenges

  • Analyze data

  • Develop innovative solutions

3. Experimental Design and Testing – Participants design and test prototypes while learning how to evaluate and refine their ideas.

4. Team Collaboration – The program emphasizes teamwork across disciplines, teaching participants how to function effectively within collaborative environments.

5. Scientific Communication – Students gain experience in presenting ideas clearly and persuasively to judges and peers.

These experiences help students develop skills that are essential for careers in engineering, technology, and scientific research.

Who Can Apply for the Young Scholars Invent Program?

Eligible Applicants

The program primarily targets high school students, specifically those in:

  • Grade 9

  • Grade 10

  • Grade 11

These students are at a critical stage where early exposure to research and engineering can significantly influence their academic and career pathways.

Eligible Countries and Regions

The program is primarily designed for students residing in the Houston area of Texas, United States, due to the in-person nature of the workshop.

However, students should always check official updates for potential expansions or special programs that may allow broader participation.

Required Qualifications

Applicants do not need extensive prior experience in programming or engineering to participate.

Basic Requirements

Eligible students should:

  • Be currently enrolled in high school (grades 9–11)

  • Demonstrate interest in STEM fields

  • Be willing to collaborate in multidisciplinary teams

  • Be prepared to participate in an intensive design workshop

  • Have parental or guardian consent (for minors)

According to the organizers, prior programming experience is not required, making the program accessible to students from diverse educational backgrounds.

Participants are encouraged to bring a laptop computer, which is used for research, design work, and creating presentations.

Skills and Knowledge Participants Will Gain

Students who complete the program gain exposure to several essential competencies that are highly valued in higher education and STEM careers.

1. STEM Knowledge Application – Participants learn how to apply knowledge from mathematics, science, and engineering to solve real-world problems.

2. Data Analysis and Experimentation – Students develop skills in:

  • Designing experiments

  • Interpreting data

  • Testing prototype designs

3. Systems Design – Participants learn how to design systems and processes that meet real-world constraints such as:

  • Environmental impact

  • Economic feasibility

  • Social considerations

  • Sustainability

4. Leadership and Collaboration – Working in teams helps students develop both leadership and teamwork capabilities.

5. Professional Communication – Students present their ideas to judges and explain the reasoning behind their designs.

Benefits of Participating in the Program

Participating in Young Scholars Invent offers numerous educational and career advantages.

1. Early Exposure to Engineering Careers – Students gain insight into what engineers and researchers actually do.

2. Networking Opportunities – Participants connect with:

  • Fellow STEM students

  • Mentors

  • Educators

  • Industry professionals

3. Hands-On Innovation Experience – Students learn the engineering design process through practice rather than theory.

4. Academic and Career Inspiration – The program helps students discover potential career paths in fields such as:

  • Engineering

  • Computer science

  • Robotics

  • Biotechnology

  • Environmental science

5. Strong Portfolio Development – Participation can strengthen applications for:

  • Universities

  • Scholarships

  • STEM competitions

  • Research programs

Structure of the Workshop

The Young Scholars Invent workshop follows a carefully structured format designed to maximize learning and engagement.

• Step 1: Introduction to the Challenge – Participants are introduced to real-world problems provided by industry partners.

• Step 2: Team Formation- Students select problems that interest them and form teams with peers from different schools.

• Step 3: Research and Concept Development – Teams research the problem and brainstorm innovative solutions.

• Step 4: Prototype Design – Participants design and test prototypes or conceptual models.

• Step 5: Final Presentation – Teams present their ideas to judges and explain how their solution addresses the challenge.

Why STEM Programs Like Young Scholars Invent Matter

Programs like Young Scholars Invent are increasingly important in a world driven by technological innovation.

Studies show that early exposure to STEM education increases students’ likelihood of pursuing careers in science and technology fields. These careers are also among the fastest-growing globally.

STEM programs help students develop critical thinking, creativity, and problem-solving abilities—skills essential for addressing global challenges such as climate change, healthcare innovation, and sustainable development.

How to Apply

Students interested in participating should:

  1. Visit the official application page provided in the reference link.

  2. Complete the application form.

  3. Submit required documentation, including parental consent forms if required.

Applicants should also regularly check the official page for announcements regarding the next application cycle and workshop dates.

Apply here

Deadline

3rd April 2026

The Young Scholars Invent program at Rice University represents an outstanding opportunity for ambitious high school students to explore engineering innovation and scientific discovery in a collaborative environment. By combining hands-on design challenges with mentorship and teamwork, the program empowers students to become creative problem-solvers capable of tackling real-world issues.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Young Scholars Invent STEM Program at Rice University 2026: A Powerful Opportunity for High School Students to Build Real-World Engineering Solutions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Elizabeth Neuffer Fellowship 2027: Fully Funded Global Fellowship for Women and Nonbinary Journalists Covering Human Rights and Social Justice
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
The Elizabeth Neuffer Fellowship 2027 is one of the most prestigious global opportunities available to journalists dedicated to reporting on human rights and social justice issues. Organized by the International Women’s Media Foundation (IWMF), this competitive program provides professional and academic training in the United States for outstanding journalists from around the world. The fellowship […]

The post Elizabeth Neuffer Fellowship 2027: Fully Funded Global Fellowship for Women and Nonbinary Journalists Covering Human Rights and Social Justice appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Elizabeth Neuffer Fellowship 2027 is one of the most prestigious global opportunities available to journalists dedicated to reporting on human rights and social justice issues. Organized by the International Women’s Media Foundation (IWMF), this competitive program provides professional and academic training in the United States for outstanding journalists from around the world.

The fellowship was established to honor the legacy of Elizabeth Neuffer, a courageous correspondent for The Boston Globe who was killed while reporting in Iraq in 2003. Through this initiative, the IWMF aims to empower journalists who are committed to exposing injustice and promoting human rights through powerful storytelling.

For journalists seeking to strengthen their investigative reporting skills, gain international exposure, and work alongside leading media institutions, this fellowship offers a life-changing opportunity.

About the Elizabeth Neuffer Fellowship

The Elizabeth Neuffer Fellowship was created by the International Women’s Media Foundation to support journalists whose work focuses on human rights, democracy, and social justice.

The program aims to:

  • Strengthen the investigative and reporting skills of journalists

  • Provide academic exposure to global political and social issues

  • Encourage impactful storytelling that exposes injustice

  • Support journalists working in challenging environments

The fellowship reflects Elizabeth Neuffer’s lifelong dedication to uncovering truth and giving voice to marginalized communities affected by conflict and human rights violations.

Benefits of the Elizabeth Neuffer Fellowship 2027

The fellowship is widely recognized as a fully funded professional development opportunity for journalists. The selected fellow receives multiple forms of support to enable full participation in the program.

Financial Support

The fellowship provides:

  • A monthly stipend to cover living expenses

  • Round-trip airfare to and from the United States

  • Housing arrangements in fellowship cities

  • Health insurance coverage

  • Transportation between fellowship locations

  • Visa assistance for international fellows

These benefits allow the fellow to focus entirely on academic research, reporting, and professional growth during the program.

Academic and Professional Training Components

The fellowship combines academic study with practical newsroom experience.

Academic Research and Study

Fellows spend part of the program at MIT’s Center for International Studies, where they can:

  • Audit courses in international relations, politics, and journalism

  • Conduct research on global human rights issues

  • Engage with leading scholars and policy experts

  • Develop analytical and investigative skills

This academic component strengthens journalists’ ability to contextualize and report complex global issues.

Newsroom Internship

Another major aspect of the fellowship is professional newsroom training. Fellows work closely with journalists at major media institutions, gaining real-world reporting experience and editorial mentorship.

This hands-on training provides opportunities to:

  • Work on investigative reporting projects

  • Publish stories under their bylines

  • Learn advanced reporting techniques

  • Build international journalism networks

Such exposure significantly enhances the fellow’s career prospects in global journalism.

Who Can Apply for the Elizabeth Neuffer Fellowship 2027

The fellowship is designed specifically for journalists who are actively reporting on issues related to human rights and social justice.

Eligible Category of Applicants

The following individuals are eligible:

  • Women journalists

  • Nonbinary journalists

  • Gender-nonconforming journalists

Applicants must be professional journalists working in:

  • Print media

  • Broadcast media

  • Digital media

  • Freelance journalism

The fellowship prioritizes journalists whose work focuses on issues such as:

  • Human rights violations

  • Social justice

  • Conflict and war reporting

  • Democracy and governance

  • Marginalized communities

Countries Eligible to Apply

One of the major strengths of this program is its global inclusivity.

Eligible Regions: Applicants from all countries worldwide can apply for the fellowship.

There are no regional restrictions. Journalists from Africa, Asia, Europe, the Americas, and Oceania are encouraged to submit applications.

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for the Elizabeth Neuffer Fellowship 2027, applicants must meet several requirements.

Professional Experience

Applicants must have:

  • At least three years of full-time professional journalism experience

Important note:

  • Internships completed during university do not count toward the required professional experience.

Language Requirements

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • Fluent English reading, writing, and speaking skills

This requirement is necessary because the fellowship activities, courses, and newsroom work are conducted in English.

Employment Status

Eligible applicants include:

  • Staff journalists working in media organizations

  • Freelance journalists producing independent journalism

Both categories are welcome to apply.

Required Application Materials

A complete fellowship application typically includes several documents demonstrating the applicant’s experience and potential.

Applicants may need to submit:

  • A professional biography

  • A current CV or resume

  • Samples of published journalism work

  • A personal statement explaining their career goals

  • Letters of recommendation

Applicants must submit their materials through the official online application portal.

Apply here

Why the Elizabeth Neuffer Fellowship Matters

The Elizabeth Neuffer Fellowship is more than a professional training program. It plays a crucial role in strengthening independent journalism worldwide.

Through this fellowship:

  • Journalists gain skills to investigate complex human rights issues

  • Stories exposing injustice reach global audiences

  • Emerging journalists develop leadership within the media industry

  • Press freedom and ethical journalism are strengthened

The fellowship also creates a global network of journalists committed to accountability, transparency, and truth.

Career Impact of the Fellowship

Many past fellows have gone on to become leading journalists, investigative reporters, and international correspondents.

The program helps fellows:

  • Publish influential investigative stories

  • Build relationships with global media institutions

  • Develop expertise in human rights reporting

  • Expand their professional networks across countries

Participation in this fellowship often significantly elevates a journalist’s career.

Host Country and Program Location

Host Country: United States
Primary Locations:

  • Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT), Cambridge, Massachusetts

  • The Boston Globe newsroom in Boston

  • Additional placement during the fellowship period in the United States

The fellowship begins with academic research and coursework at MIT’s Center for International Studies and includes professional newsroom experience with major media organizations.

Fellowship Duration and Timeline

The fellowship lasts approximately six months, offering a combination of academic and professional development.

Fellowship Schedule

  • June 2026: Fellow selected

  • February – May 2027: Academic research and coursework at MIT and internship in Boston

  • June – July 2027: Additional professional placement in the United States

  • July 2027: Fellowship concludes

During this time, the selected fellow engages in research, journalism training, and hands-on reporting experience.

Deadline

19th April 2026

The Elizabeth Neuffer Fellowship 2027 represents a rare opportunity for journalists passionate about human rights reporting to gain world-class training and international recognition. With its combination of academic research at MIT, newsroom experience with leading media organizations, and full financial support, the fellowship equips journalists with the tools necessary to produce impactful journalism that holds power accountable.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Elizabeth Neuffer Fellowship 2027: Fully Funded Global Fellowship for Women and Nonbinary Journalists Covering Human Rights and Social Justice appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: 12th Youth Leadership Workshop on Global Citizenship Education (GCED) 2026 in Seoul, South Korea (Fully Funded): Apply by 26 March 2026
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The 12th Youth Leadership Workshop on Global Citizenship Education (GCED) 2026 is now open for applications, offering an exceptional fully funded opportunity for young leaders from developing countries to participate in an international leadership workshop in Seoul, Republic of Korea. Organized by the Asia-Pacific Centre of Education for International Understanding (APCEIU) under the auspices of […]

The post 12th Youth Leadership Workshop on Global Citizenship Education (GCED) 2026 in Seoul, South Korea (Fully Funded): Apply by 26 March 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The 12th Youth Leadership Workshop on Global Citizenship Education (GCED) 2026 is now open for applications, offering an exceptional fully funded opportunity for young leaders from developing countries to participate in an international leadership workshop in Seoul, Republic of Korea.

Organized by the Asia-Pacific Centre of Education for International Understanding (APCEIU) under the auspices of UNESCO, in partnership with the UNESCO Mahatma Gandhi Institute of Education for Peace and Sustainable Development (MGIEP) and the GCED Youth Network, this prestigious program aims to equip youth leaders with the skills, knowledge, and networks needed to promote peace, advocacy, and social transformation through media and the arts.

Under the theme “Translating Peace into Action: Leveraging Media and the Arts for Youth Advocacy,” the workshop will empower young changemakers to critically engage with global issues, develop innovative advocacy strategies, and contribute meaningfully to advancing peace and sustainable development within their communities.

The program will bring together 40 young leaders from Official Development Assistance (ODA) recipient countries, offering a platform for collaboration, learning, and global networking.

About the Youth Leadership Workshop on GCED

The Youth Leadership Workshop on Global Citizenship Education (GCED) is one of UNESCO APCEIU’s flagship initiatives aimed at strengthening youth capacity in peacebuilding, advocacy, and global citizenship education.

The workshop is designed to:

  • Promote awareness and understanding of Global Citizenship Education

  • Encourage youth-led advocacy and community engagement

  • Strengthen young leaders’ abilities to leverage media, arts, and storytelling for social change

  • Provide international exposure and networking opportunities

  • Support participants in developing initiatives that advance peace, justice, and sustainable development

The workshop also connects participants with a global network of youth advocates and educators working on GCED initiatives worldwide.

Theme of the 2026 Workshop

The theme for the 12th Youth Leadership Workshop is:

“Translating Peace into Action: Leveraging Media and the Arts for Youth Advocacy.”

This theme focuses on the role of creative expression and communication tools in advancing peace and social justice.

Participants will explore how to:

  • Use media platforms and storytelling for advocacy

  • Utilize art and culture as tools for social transformation

  • Promote peace narratives and intercultural understanding

  • Engage communities in dialogue around global issues

  • Develop youth-led advocacy initiatives for lasting change

Why You Should Apply

The 12th Youth Leadership Workshop on GCED offers a rare opportunity for young leaders to:

  • Receive international leadership training

  • Develop advocacy skills through media and arts

  • Join a global network of youth changemakers

  • Learn from global experts in peace education

  • Participate in a fully funded international program in South Korea

  • Gain access to mentorship opportunities for youth-led initiatives

Participants will return to their communities equipped with tools and knowledge to promote peace, dialogue, and sustainable development.

Workshop Schedule and Format

The workshop includes both online and in-person components, allowing participants to prepare and engage effectively before attending the main international gathering.

Pre-Workshop (Online)

  • Date: 12–14 May 2026

  • Participants will participate in online sessions designed to introduce workshop themes and prepare for in-person collaboration.

Main Workshop (In-Person)

  • Date: 8–12 June 2026

  • Location: Seoul, Republic of Korea

During the in-person program, participants will take part in:

  • Leadership training sessions

  • Interactive workshops

  • Youth advocacy discussions

  • Media and arts-based activities

  • Networking sessions with global youth leaders and experts

Financial Coverage and Benefits

Selected participants will receive full financial support to attend the workshop in South Korea.

The program covers:

  • Round-trip international airfare to and from Seoul

  • Accommodation during the workshop

  • Meals throughout the program

  • Local transportation in Korea

  • Access to training sessions and workshop materials

  • Participation in international networking opportunities

This ensures that participants from developing countries can attend the program without financial barriers.

Mentorship Opportunities After the Workshop

Participants who successfully complete the workshop will become eligible to apply for an exclusive mentorship program designed to support youth-led initiatives.

The mentorship component aims to:

  • Support participants in implementing their advocacy projects

  • Provide expert guidance and coaching

  • Strengthen project sustainability

  • Expand impact within participants’ communities

Prerequisite Course Requirement

Applicants must complete a mandatory prerequisite online course before submitting their application.

About the Course

The prerequisite course provides foundational knowledge on:

  • Global Citizenship Education (GCED)

  • Social and Emotional Learning (SEL)

  • Youth advocacy and leadership

Course Features

  • Estimated duration: 3–4 hours

  • Format: Self-paced

  • Cost: 100% free

  • Certificate issued upon completion

The course completion certificate must be uploaded with the application.

Applications submitted without the certificate will not be considered valid.

Application Requirements

Applicants must complete the online application form and submit the following materials:

  1. Fully completed application form with personal information and motivation

  2. Curriculum Vitae (CV) or resume

  3. One-minute self-introduction video

  4. Certificate of completion from the prerequisite GCED course

Applications must be submitted before the deadline to be considered.

Application Timeline

  • Application Period: 12 March 2026 – 26 March 2026

  • Prerequisite Course Period: 12 March 2026 – 26 March 2026

  • Application Deadline: 26 March 2026, 23:59 (KST)

  • Results Notification: 8 April 2026, 23:59 (KST)

Selected participants will then prepare for the online pre-workshop sessions in May 2026 and the in-person workshop in Seoul in June 2026.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following conditions:

  • Be a young leader actively engaged in Global Citizenship Education activities

  • Be from an Official Development Assistance (ODA) recipient country

  • Demonstrate commitment to peacebuilding, advocacy, and social change

  • Be willing to participate fully in both the online and in-person components of the workshop

  • Successfully complete the prerequisite GCED course

Only 40 participants will be selected globally.

How to Apply

Interested applicants must complete the official application form online and upload all required documents.

Application Link:
https://forms.gle/Y4iDdsepanztxbxN7

Applicants should also complete the prerequisite GCED course before submitting the application.

Contact Information

For inquiries regarding the workshop or course:

Course-related inquiries:
gcedonline@unescoapceiu.org

Technical issues with file uploads:
training@unescoapceiu.org

For additional information, applicants may also contact:
youth.mgiep@unesco.org

Read more about the 12th Youth Leadership Workshop on GCED 2026 HERE.

Click HERE to discover more other global conference/workshop opportunities on OFY.

The post 12th Youth Leadership Workshop on Global Citizenship Education (GCED) 2026 in Seoul, South Korea (Fully Funded): Apply by 26 March 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded Scholar Rescue Fund Fellowship 2026: A Global Lifeline for Threatened Scholars and Academics Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Institute of International Education Scholar Rescue Fund Fellowship (IIE-SRF) is one of the most important international opportunities designed to support scholars whose academic work and personal safety are under threat in their home countries. Managed by the Institute of International Education, this prestigious program provides temporary academic placements and financial support to ensure that […]

The post Fully Funded Scholar Rescue Fund Fellowship 2026: A Global Lifeline for Threatened Scholars and Academics Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Institute of International Education Scholar Rescue Fund Fellowship (IIE-SRF) is one of the most important international opportunities designed to support scholars whose academic work and personal safety are under threat in their home countries. Managed by the Institute of International Education, this prestigious program provides temporary academic placements and financial support to ensure that scholars facing persecution, conflict, or repression can continue their teaching and research in a safe academic environment.

Since its establishment, the program has become a vital global initiative dedicated to protecting intellectual freedom and preserving academic knowledge by assisting scholars who would otherwise be unable to continue their work. More than 1,000 scholars from over 60 countries have already benefited from the fellowship, with placements in hundreds of universities across the world.

About the Scholar Rescue Fund Fellowship

The Scholar Rescue Fund Fellowship is the only global program dedicated exclusively to arranging and funding fellowships for threatened and displaced scholars. The fellowship offers scholars an opportunity to temporarily relocate to host universities around the world where they can continue their academic work safely.

Many academics around the world face severe threats due to their research, political climate, armed conflicts, or persecution for their ideas. These threats often make it impossible for them to continue teaching, conducting research, or publishing in their home countries.

The fellowship program addresses this challenge by providing:

  • Safe academic placement at international institutions

  • Financial support to sustain research and teaching

  • Opportunities to maintain academic careers

  • Support for intellectual freedom and academic independence

In many cases, fellows continue contributing to global scholarship while maintaining ties to their home regions and academic communities.
